req.title: Close encounter
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct testing concerns: (1) the happy-path state transition from 'in-progress' to 'finished' via POST /Encounter/{id}/close, (2) the rejection of further modifications on a closed encounter (negative/error path returning 409), and (3) the boundary condition where an addendum Observation is the sole permitted write after closure. State Transition Coverage drives the happy-path and addendum tests because the system moves through well-defined states (open → finished). Negative Path and Decision Table drive the rejection tests. Equivalence Partitioning isolates the 'closed encounter' partition for the modification-rejection test. BVA targets the exact threshold between permitted (addendum) and forbidden (non-addendum) writes on a closed encounter.

tests.name: test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished
preconditions: The acting user has clinician role
actions: Create a fresh open Encounter via <create_encounter_fixture>
GET /Encounter/{id} to read back the resource
trace: R-HLT-007: 'A clinician shall close an Encounter'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished(base_url, clinician_headers, open_encounter):
    enc_id = open_encounter["id"]

    close_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200, close_resp.text

    body = close_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "finished", (
        f"Expected 'finished', got {body.get('status')!r}"
    )

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"

tests.name: test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: An Encounter exists with status='finished' (already closed)
The acting user has clinician role
actions: Create and close an Encounter via <create_and_close_encounter_fixture>
Attempt PUT /Encounter/{id} with a modified non-addendum field (e.g., change 'period.end')
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating the encounter is closed
trace: R-HLT-007: 'closed Encounters shall reject further modifications'
R-HLT-007: error pathway '409 when modifying closed encounter'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409(base_url, clinician_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    modified = dict(closed_encounter)
    modified["period"] = {"end": "2099-12-31T23:59:59Z"}

    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        json=modified,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409 Conflict, got {put_resp.status_code}: {put_resp.text}"
    )

    error_body = put_resp.json()
    assert "closed" in str(error_body).lower() or "finished" in str(error_body).lower()

tests.name: test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter
preconditions: The acting user has clinician role
actions: Create and close an Encounter via <create_and_close_encounter_fixture>
POST an Observation with category='addendum' linked to the closed Encounter
GET the Observation to verify persistence
expected: Observation is linked to the closed Encounter
trace: R-HLT-007: 'closed Encounters shall reject further modifications EXCEPT for addendum Observations'
R-HLT-007: boundary between permitted and forbidden writes on a closed encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter(base_url, clinician_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    addendum_obs = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "addendum"}]}],
        "code": {"text": "Addendum note"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
    }

    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=addendum_obs,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Expected 201 for addendum Observation, got {post_resp.status_code}: {post_resp.text}"
    )

    obs_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    get_obs = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_headers)
    assert get_obs.status_code == 200
    assert f"Encounter/{enc_id}" in get_obs.json()["encounter"]["reference"]

    enc_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}", headers=clinician_headers)
    assert enc_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"

tests.name: test_close_already_closed_encounter_returns_conflict
preconditions: The acting user has clinician role
actions: Create and close an Encounter via <create_and_close_encounter_fixture>
POST /Encounter/{id}/close a second time on the already-closed Encounter
expected: Response body signals that the encounter is already in a terminal state
trace: R-HLT-007: state (finished) x action (close) -> error, per Decision Table row
R-HLT-007: 'closed Encounters shall reject further modifications'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_close_already_closed_encounter_returns_conflict(base_url, clinician_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    second_close = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert second_close.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409 on double-close, got {second_close.status_code}: {second_close.text}"
    )

    error_body = second_close.json()
    detail = str(error_body).lower()
    assert "finished" in detail or "already closed" in detail or "conflict" in detail

tests.name: test_non_addendum_observation_on_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: The acting user has clinician role
actions: Create and close an Encounter via <create_and_close_encounter_fixture>
POST an Observation with category='exam' (non-addendum) linked to the closed Encounter
expected: Non-addendum writes are rejected on a finished encounter
trace: R-HLT-007: only addendum Observations are exempted; all other writes are forbidden
R-HLT-007: error pathway '409 when modifying closed encounter'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_addendum_observation_on_closed_encounter_returns_409(base_url, clinician_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    non_addendum_obs = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "exam"}]}],
        "code": {"text": "Routine exam note"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
    }

    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=non_addendum_obs,
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409 for non-addendum Observation on closed encounter, "
        f"got {post_resp.status_code}: {post_resp.text}"
    )
